ApplyResponse.description:
The response to a completion submission (D-072).

Exactly one of ``next_envelope`` (when ``outcome == next``) or ``detail`` (on a
non-advancing outcome) is meaningful; the ``outcome`` discriminates.
ApplyResponse.title: ApplyResponse

ArtifactView.description:
One entry of a chunk's inline artifact store (D-036).

``key`` is the store key ``{node}.{artifact-name}.{epoch}`` — append-only, so
every re-run's entry is retained and latest-by-epoch resolution is the reader's
(D-089). ``content`` carries an **asset's** text verbatim (a review's findings
document); the ``repo``/``branch_name``/``commit_hash`` trio carries a
``git_commit`` artifact's pinned reference (the hub stores the reference, never the
code — D-012).
ArtifactView.title: ArtifactView

CheckResult.description: One deterministic check's outcome, informing the verdict (D-077).
CheckResult.title: CheckResult

ChunkDetail.description:
The chunk aggregate in full (D-036) — the board's chunk view and envelope feed.

Carries the chunk's **transition history** and its inline **artifact store** so the
web app can render every node it visited, the review that failed once and looped
back to build, and the artifacts — the branch pointers merged and the review notes
(product/mvp.md, MVP criterion 9/11).
ChunkDetail.title: ChunkDetail

ChunkIngestRequest.description: Ingest by pointer — specific items always, batch fine (D-047).
ChunkIngestRequest.title: ChunkIngestRequest

ChunkStatus.description: The derived chunk statuses (D-067). Never stored — always a query result.
ChunkStatus.title: ChunkStatus

ChunkSummary.description: One row of the fleet chunk list — derived status + current node (D-004).
ChunkSummary.title: ChunkSummary

CompletionSubmission.description: A node-step's completion — judgement choice + checks + artifacts + epoch.
CompletionSubmission.title: CompletionSubmission

EnvelopeArtifact.description: One artifact carried into a node-step, resolved latest-by-epoch (D-036).
EnvelopeArtifact.title: EnvelopeArtifact

EnvelopeChoice.description: A selectable outcome the worker's judgement may emit (D-042).
EnvelopeChoice.title: EnvelopeChoice

EscalationReport.description:
A runner's ``escalation.recorded`` — retries exhausted for the node (D-009).

``takeover_command`` is the literal ``cd <workdir> && <harness resume>`` a human
pastes to enter the parked session (design/harness-adapters.md); ``epoch`` is the
exhausted attempt's fence, closed by a later lease mint (D-067).
EscalationReport.title: EscalationReport

EscalationView.description:
An open escalation on a ``needs_human`` chunk (D-009/D-067).

Surfaces the runner-composed takeover command so a human can resume the parked
session (design/harness-adapters.md). Present only while the escalation is open —
a later lease mint (requeue/takeover) supersedes it and this drops away (D-067).
EscalationView.title: EscalationView

GraphMintRequest.description: A graph definition to mint — the raw YAML body (D-071).
GraphMintRequest.title: GraphMintRequest

GraphNodeView.description: A reified node in a minted graph.
GraphNodeView.title: GraphNodeView

GraphView.description: A minted graph as served by ``GET /graphs`` and the mint response.
GraphView.title: GraphView

JudgedBy.description: Who renders a node's exit judgement — the structural gate marker (D-041).
JudgedBy.title: JudgedBy

LeaseMintReport.description: A runner's ``lease.minted`` — one node-step attempt's fencing epoch (D-044).
LeaseMintReport.title: LeaseMintReport

NodeConfig.description: The node's invariant identity for this step (D-025/D-038).
NodeConfig.title: NodeConfig

NodeEnvelope.description: Everything a runner needs to work one node-step (D-089).
NodeEnvelope.title: NodeEnvelope

PmItemView.description: A pass-through PM item read (D-047) — body + comments, vendor-native.
PmItemView.title: PmItemView

QueuePeekEntry.description: One ready chunk, in queue order.
QueuePeekEntry.title: QueuePeekEntry

QueuePeekResponse.description: The ready queue as peeked by FILL.
QueuePeekResponse.title: QueuePeekResponse

ReadinessResponse.description: The wire shape of a readiness reading (openapi-ts consumes this).
ReadinessResponse.title: ReadinessResponse

RouteClaim.description: A complete route fact posted by the claiming runner (D-080).
RouteClaim.title: RouteClaim

RouteClaimResponse.description: The winning claim's reply — the route plus the first node envelope.
RouteClaimResponse.title: RouteClaimResponse

RouteView.description: A chunk's route — where it is being worked (D-021).
RouteView.title: RouteView

RunnerFact.description:
One buffered runner fact: its per-runner seq, its kind, and its payload.

``payload`` is the kind-specific body — for ``lease.minted`` ``{chunk_id, epoch}``,
for ``escalation.recorded`` ``{chunk_id, epoch, takeover_command}`` — kept open so a
new runner fact kind bolts on without a wire change.
RunnerFact.title: RunnerFact

RunnerFactAck.description:
The hub's per-batch acknowledgement against its high-water mark.

``high_water`` is the runner's new mark after this batch; ``applied`` and
``already_applied`` partition the pushed seqs so the runner can ack its buffer
(a semantic rejection still acks — rejection is an outcome, not a delivery
failure, D-069). ``rejected`` names seqs the hub refused for a non-idempotency
reason (an unknown kind), which the runner surfaces rather than silently drops.
RunnerFactAck.title: RunnerFactAck

RunnerFactBatch.description: A runner's push of one-or-more buffered facts, ordered by seq (D-069).
RunnerFactBatch.title: RunnerFactBatch

SubmittedArtifact.description: An artifact committed atomically with the completion (D-036).
SubmittedArtifact.title: SubmittedArtifact

TransitionView.description:
One accepted transition in a chunk's history (D-027/D-036).

The edge a node-step took — its origin node, the judgement choice that routed it,
and its destination — oldest first on the detail. This is what makes the review-fail
loop legible: a ``review -> build`` entry with ``choice_name = "fail"`` is a visible
step in the timeline (MVP criterion 9/11).
TransitionView.title: TransitionView

get.description: The fleet chunk list — derived status per chunk (D-004).

schema.title: Response List Chunks Api Chunks Get

post.description: Ingest by pointer (D-047); 409 on a pointer held by a live chunk (D-093).

get.description: One chunk aggregate in full — derived status, current node, route (D-036).

post.description: Apply a node-step's completion atomically; reply carries the next envelope (D-072).

get.description: The chunk's current node envelope, idempotent — the lost-apply re-read (D-090).

post.description: Land a runner's ``escalation.recorded`` — the chunk derives ``needs_human`` (D-009).

schema.title: Response Report Escalation Api Chunks  Chunk Id  Escalations Post

post.description: Land a runner's ``lease.minted`` — keeps the epoch fence in lockstep (D-044).

schema.title: Response Report Lease Api Chunks  Chunk Id  Leases Post

get.description: Pass-through PM item read — body + comments, contents never stored (D-047).

post.description:
Land runner-minted facts, idempotent by per-runner seq high-water (D-069/D-044).

The store-and-forward ingest: ``lease.minted`` (the fence input) and
``escalation.recorded`` ride the runner's outbound buffer here. A pushed seq at or
below the runner's high-water mark is already-applied and re-acked; a fresh one is
applied and advances the mark. Emits ``chunk-changed`` for each chunk a landed
fact touched so the board refreshes.
Validate and mint an immutable graph; 422 on validation errors (D-071).
Claim a chunk; 409 if already claimed, else the first node envelope (D-080).
